signals.title: USD/NZD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Economic Surprise
signals.text: USD/NZD at 0.5865 — USD outperformance dominates the G8 complex following upbeat US economic data and a hawkish shift in Fed leadership under Kevin Warsh. USD CESI net +25 beats confirms a sustained data-outperformance regime vs NZD neutral data. Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25% provides a +150bp carry advantage to the USD, further supported by a +1.23% US real rate vs -0.83% NZD real rate. The 1.558% G8 spread marks a strong directional theme.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Technical + Carry + Options
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.034 — price has pushed above the psychologically critical 160.00 level, a zone previously associated with BoJ verbal warnings and intervention risk. Despite the break, the +292bp SOFR-TONA carry differential sustains the bid. Options market remains cautious of a reversal with a -1.18 25d RR put skew, signaling protection buying against JPY strength despite the spot extension. Intervention risk rises on a sustained hold above 160.00.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7134 — COT LF net long +58,041 (76.1% long) vs retail 73% short creates a sharp institutional vs retail divergence. While specs are positioned for AUD strength, disappointing Australian GDP and firm US data have triggered a -0.68% intraday decline. Elevated vol premium (VRP +7.1%) indicates the market is paying for protection. If price holds below 0.7100, a crowded spec long unwind accelerates the move lower.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Volatility Premium + Carry
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7923 — IV at 31.9% vs HV30 at 6.6% creates an extreme +25.3% Vol Risk Premium, signaling the options market is pricing in massive tail-risk protection relative to realized movement. Fed 3.75% vs SNB 0.0% provides a +375bp carry advantage to USD, but the VRP extreme suggests hedging against a sharp CHF safe-haven bid or SNB policy shift. Carry remains the dominant driver while VIX stays at 16.1 (normal volatility).

signals.title: GBP/USD — Carry Neutral + Options Skew
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3416 — Fed 3.75% vs BoE 3.75% results in a 0bp carry differential, leaving the pair sensitive to macro headlines and yield curve shifts. The US bear steepener (+51.1bp 2s10s) supports the USD leg. Options market shows a -0.56 25d RR put skew, indicating a mild bias for GBP weakness. Watch the 1.3400 level; a break targets the 1M low as US data outperformance continues.
